code:
xml
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1★ (Very Negative):
    - Complete failure of core functionality despite proper use
    - Multi-purchase defects with safety risks
    - Usability failures making product dangerous/impractical
    - Negative updates overriding initial satisfaction

    2★ (Negative):
    - Partial functionality requiring constant adjustments
    - Clearly inferior to alternatives in critical aspects
    - "Regret purchase" statements with justification
    - Persistent frustration despite occasional success

    3★ (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual limitations
    - Adequate for specific use cases with compromises
    - Conditional recommendations without endorsement
    - Technical competence with notable drawbacks

    4★ (Positive):
    - Single functional flaw impacting core experience
    - Explicit star deduction phrases ("only reason not 5")
    - Brief positive statements lacking enthusiasm ("works fine")
    - Assembly difficulties despite final satisfaction
    - Requires break-in period for full functionality

    5★ (Very Positive):
    - Concise praise indicating satisfaction without negatives
    - 3+ functional aspects praised (durability/design/utility)
    - Close brand comparisons with satisfied performance (+minor differences)
    - Non-functional criticism WITH enthusiastic core praise
    - Solves persistent problems exceeding expectations
    - Exceeds initial skepticism with demonstrated effectiveness
    - Maintenance inherent to product type (e.g., cast iron care)

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - "Works as intended" →5 unless functional criticism exists
    - Functional praise + non-functional issue →5
    - Functional praise + functional flaw →4
    - Multi-purchase failures →1 regardless of initial rating
    - Setup difficulties →5 if no lingering functional impact
    - "Nearly matches X" →5 if satisfaction expressed
    - Statements indicating mere adequacy →4
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "Second unit failed faster. Dangerous defects!"
<Reasoning>Multi-purchase safety failures override positives →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Does exactly what needed - deep pockets fit any mattress"
<Reasoning>Concise functional praise without negatives →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Nearly matches Lodge! Daily driver despite minor differences"
<Reasoning>Close brand comparison with satisfaction →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "Skeptical at first, but cleans better than any previous vacuum!"
<Reasoning>Exceeded low expectations with effectiveness →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "This skillet requires seasoning but cooks perfectly. Lasts generations!"
<Reasoning>Inherent maintenance with core functionality praise →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Works fine for occasional use. Not heavy-duty."
<Reasoning>Adequate with clear limitations →3</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Constant slipping ruins the experience. Avoid!"
<Reasoning>Persistent functional frustration →2</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Great concept fails in practice - parts detach during use"
<Reasoning>Core usability failure →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "Vibrant colors fade over time but initial quality excellent"
<Reasoning>Non-functional drawback with core praise →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "Does the job adequately. Nothing special but functional"
<Reasoning>Lack of enthusiasm indicates adequacy →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
artifacts_json:

  gold 5 -> 4: 8
  gold 4 -> 3: 3
  gold 1 -> 4: 1
  gold 5 -> 1: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):

     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "There is only one problem for the experienced cook when it comes to this set - you're likely to already have one or more of these tools and possibly Oxo Good Grips at that! That's why I think this set is best suited for someone setting up a kitchen f..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "UPDATE
==================================

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "I like the Stasher Snack bag. It is the perfect size for snacks on the go. I also like that you can store, cook, put in the microwave or even in the dishwasher! This is great for small treats. Don't try to overstuff. You don't want the seal to get fo..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "Between a puppy and frequent visits from children, I've definitely had times when it felt like the gap between proper carpet cleanings was entirely too long. On the other hand, I never entirely bought the idea that a device of this type could turn ou..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I have only used this once so far, but I really like it. It is easy to operate once you figure out the steam buttons...lol! The scrubber was effective and it cleaned a pretty sticky kitchen floor. Next..on to my tile."

  7. Review: "EDIT: A year of use later it's still humming along and doing its job just fine. Sometimes it seems like it doesn't pick up quickly that it should be upping its speed on its own in the auto setting, but maybe it's just taking longer for the air to rea..."

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "It's a very solid bed that feels like it's going to hold up to just about anything. Assembly wasn't too difficult, though there were a couple of parts that had to be under some pressure to get the screw holes to line up just right. Also, when we firs..."

  2. Review: "This was pretty straightforward to put together, feels very sturdy, leaves lots of room underneath for storage (plus a little space I left open because the dog seems to be happy about being able to curl up under there and use it as a hiding place), a..."

  3. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"

Summary: 27 errors, 23 borderline out of 80 examples."}
